message:
chore(beads): issue tracker status updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3284,7 +3284,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.3","title":"[asupersync][vendored] wezterm-ssh and remote transport migration","description":"Migrate wezterm-ssh and related remote transport paths to asupersync-compatible behavior and verify remote-session resilience.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Runtime-incompatible remote transport primitives are replaced or wrapped with doctrine-compliant seams.\n2. Remote connection lifecycle, retry, and cancellation behavior are deterministic and documented.\n3. Unit tests cover transport state machine and error paths.\n4. Integration tests validate end-to-end remote workflows.\n5. E2E scripts run remote-session scenarios with rich structured logs/artifacts.","notes":"Remote paths are operationally sensitive; treat regressions here as high severity. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.995448Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:48:18.795295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","ssh","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-brc7d.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-brc7d.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Core↔vendored async contract and compatibility tests","description":"Define and enforce async contract boundaries between core and vendored layers (ownership, cancellation, channeling, error mapping).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Publish contract matrix for core↔vendored async interactions.\n2. Add contract tests that fail on semantic drift.\n3. Unit tests cover boundary adapters and invariant checks.\n4. Integration tests validate cross-layer workflows and degraded behavior.\n5. E2E scripts prove no cross-layer runtime semantic mismatch with structured logs.","notes":"This is where cohesion is won or lost. Make boundaries explicit and test-enforced. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayStone","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:20.588804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:49:40.114718Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","contracts","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1610,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","author":"GrayStone","text":"Progress 2026-02-27: runtime_compat asupersync seam advanced. Added asupersync select/join compatibility in runtime_compat (runtime_compat::select! path now available under asupersync), fixed Notify::notified API mismatch, switched web.rs + web/sse.rs off direct tokio::select/signal usage, and updated main.rs shutdown channel send to runtime_compat::mpsc_send. Validation via rch: cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features asupersync-runtime ✅; cargo check -p frankenterm --all-targets --features asupersync-runtime ✅; cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets --features asupersync-runtime still fails on broader pre-existing asupersync-test migration surfaces (runtime_compat::time cfg and mpsc/watch Cx callsites in tests).","created_at":"2026-02-28T00:01:13Z"},{"id":1654,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","author":"NavyBadger","text":"Validation note: running asupersync feature checks/tests via rch surfaced pre-existing compile/test-gating debt in runtime_compat and related tests (e.g. runtime_compat::join/select/time and signal/JoinError paths) unrelated to new snapshot trigger tests. Suggest consolidating feature cfg seams before broad --all-targets asupersync test runs.","created_at":"2026-02-28T23:49:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","title":"[asupersync][track] Deterministic verification and deep testing","description":"Build proof-oriented validation for correctness under concurrency and cancellation.

Scope:
- LabRuntime deterministic suites
- DPOR/failure injection
- full-stack integration + e2e evidence packs

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit and deterministic component test suites cover critical async state machines and cancellation paths.\n2. Concurrency exploration/failure injection catches race, deadlock, and message-loss regressions.\n3. Integration and e2e suites validate cross-crate runtime behavior under stress/degradation.\n4. Crash/restart persistence invariants are explicitly validated for capture/session/search/control-plane state with replayable evidence.\n5. Test output includes machine-parseable artifacts plus rich, detailed structured logs (with correlation IDs and failure context) for debugging.\n6. Validate a mandatory user workflow matrix (human CLI + robot mode + watch + remote paths) with deterministic pass/fail artifacts.\n7. Heavy compile/test workloads are run via rch exec -- <command> with evidence recorded.","notes":"This track turns runtime migration from trust-me to verifiable engineering. Determinism, user-journey proof, and artifact quality are mandatory for confidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. This bead must include cross-module integration tests that validate boundary behavior, data contracts, and degraded/failure interactions under representative workloads. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"OliveMoose","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:24:38.130122Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T04:46:32.319894Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","labruntime","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.1","title":"[asupersync][verify] LabRuntime deterministic suites for critical state machines","description":"Build deterministic LabRuntime suites for high-risk async state machines (daemon loops, IPC handlers, schedulers, shutdown flows).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic tests cover critical state transitions and cancellation checkpoints.\n2. Priority state machines include user-critical paths (robot command loops, watch ingest loops, session lifecycle, shutdown/restart).\n3. Tests are seed-controlled and reproducible with artifact capture.\n4. Unit tests validate harness helpers and deterministic clocks/schedulers.\n5. Integration tests execute full state-machine flows under deterministic runtime.\n6. E2E script hooks include deterministic replay metadata and structured logs.","notes":"Determinism reduces triage cost and catches edge races early. Prioritize high-impact state machines that users feel first. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCat","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:21.178718Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T18:44:01.329301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","labruntime","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1527,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.1","author":"OliveMoose","text":"Progress update (OliveMoose): implemented deterministic LabRuntime/DPOR verification slice for critical state-machine paths.

Code added:
1. `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tailer_labruntime.rs`
   - `lab_tailer_sync_handles_pane_restart_without_resurrecting_removed_pane`
   - Covers ingest loop lifecycle transition (pane removed + new pane introduced), verifies no stale resurrection and deterministic metrics (`events_sent`, `no_change_captures`).
2. `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/distributed_merge_dpor.rs`
   - `dpor_distributed_reconnect_replay_preserves_contiguous_sequence`
   - Covers IPC/distributed reconnect with overlapping replay window; asserts contiguous accepted sequence (1..=12) without gaps/duplication across interleavings.
3. `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/web_streaming_dpor.rs`
   - `dpor_stream_reconnect_receives_ordered_suffix_after_restart`
   - Covers scheduler/subscriber lifecycle: disconnect + reconnect; verifies restarted subscriber receives clean ordered suffix only.
4. `tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_6_1.sh`
   - Added structured JSONL e2e harness with scenario/correlation metadata and strict `rch` offload guard (fails on local fallback).

Validation status:
- `bash -n tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_6_1.sh` passes.
- Full heavy validation is currently blocked by unavailable rch workers.
  - `rch workers probe --all --json` reports all workers `connection_failed`.
  - e2e preflight artifact paths:
    - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260225_095602.jsonl`
    - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260225_095602_rch_probe.json`
    - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260225_095602.stdout.log`

Next action once rch is healthy: run the new e2e harness and the required cargo check/clippy/fmt gates via `rch exec -- ...` only.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T09:59:21Z"},{"id":1574,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.1","author":"WildCat","text":"WildCat stale-handoff slice update (2026-02-26): hardened `tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_6_1.sh` preflight diagnostics and offload safety.

Changes:
1. Added `rch check` preflight artifact capture and explicit preflight event (`rch_check_ready`).
2. Added `rch --json status --workers --jobs` fallback telemetry when `rch workers probe --all --json` shows zero reachable workers.
3. Added explicit mismatch/error taxonomy:
   - `reason_code=rch_health_probe_mismatch`, `error_code=RCH-E101`
4. Enforced strict policy: probe=0 reachable now always fails preflight (even if status reports healthy) to prevent accidental local fail-open compilation.
5. Made `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` run-unique (`...-${RUN_ID}`) to reduce target-dir contention.

Validation:
- `bash -n tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_6_1.sh` PASS
- Live run: `tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_6_1.sh` exits `2` with deterministic mismatch classification.
- Latest artifacts:
  - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260226_184329.jsonl`
  - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260226_184329_rch_check.log`
  - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260226_184329_rch_probe.json`
  - `tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_6_1_20260226_184329_rch_status.json`

Current blocker remains infra-level remote-execution instability (probe reports 0 reachable despite check/status health signals).
","created_at":"2026-02-26T18:44:01Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.2","title":"[asupersync][verify] DPOR + concurrency failure injection matrix","description":"Introduce systematic concurrency exploration and failure injection for race, deadlock, and cancellation-loss classes.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define failure matrix covering cancellation storms, backpressure spikes, timeout races, and partial I/O failures.\n2. Matrix includes explicit user-facing scenarios (rate-limit waits, reconnect storms, remote command retries, startup under degraded I/O).\n3. Automate DPOR/concurrency exploration where applicable.\n4. Unit tests validate failure-injection controls and expected assertions.\n5. Integration tests run matrix scenarios in CI-friendly slices.\n6.
